author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
strī⋅puruṣa⋅dehagata, varṇa⋅ādi dharma jata,
tāhe punaḥ dehagata bheda
sattvarajastamoguṇa, āśrayete bheda punaḥ,
eirūpa sahasra prabheda

je⋅kona śarīre thāki, je⋅kona avasthā rākhi,
se⋅saba ekhana tava pā⋅ya
sa̐pilāma, prāṇeśvara! mama bali’ ataḥpara,
āra kichu nā rahila dāya

tumi, prabhu! rākha māra, saba tava adhikāra,
āchi āmi tomāra kiṅkara
e bhaktivinoda bale, tava dāsya kautūhale,
thāki jena sadā sevāpara
en_translation:
O Hari! All the duties related to male and female bodies, social orders, and so forth; the distinctions based on the body itself; and further differences due to the qualities of goodness, passion, and ignorance—in this way, there are thousands of divisions.

Whatever body I may inhabit, whatever state I may be in, all of that I now surrender at Your feet, O Prāṇeśvara! From now on, nothing remains for me to call "mine" or to be my responsibility.

You, my Lord! You may protect or destroy; everything is under Your control. I am Your servant. Bhaktivinoda says: out of eagerness for Your service, may I always remain engaged in serving You.
